# nano-benchmark

> Command-line utilities for micro-benchmarking JavaScript code with nonparametric statistics and significance testing.

## Overview

nano-benchmark provides three CLI tools for benchmarking code. It uses nonparametric statistics (bootstrap resampling, quantile-based confidence intervals) and rank-based significance tests (Mann-Whitney U for two functions; Kruskal-Wallis H with Conover-Iman pairwise post-hoc for three or more, with Holm/Bonferroni multiple-comparison correction) to produce statistically rigorous results. Saved runs can be re-rendered and compared with significance recomputed from the raw samples. Designed for micro-benchmarks where function-call overhead matters.

- NPM: https://npmjs.org/package/nano-benchmark
- GitHub: https://github.com/uhop/nano-bench
- Wiki: https://github.com/uhop/nano-bench/wiki
- License: BSD-3-Clause
- Runtime: Node.js (every non-EOL release), Bun, Deno; no `engines` floor pinned
- Module system: ESM only (`"type": "module"`)

## Installation

```bash
npm install nano-benchmark
```

## CLI tools

### nano-bench

Benchmarks multiple functions, compares them with bootstrap confidence intervals and significance tests, outputs a styled table. Name one or more methods after the file to benchmark just those; a single name runs as a baseline (no significance test).

```bash
npx nano-bench benchmark.js
npx nano-bench -s 200 -b 2000 -a 0.01 benchmark.js
npx nano-bench benchmark.js --json out.json --label after-opt   # save results
npx nano-bench benchmark.js --histogram --chart bars            # show distributions
```

Options:
- Measurement: `--ms` (time per sample, default 50), `--iterations` (fixed count, overrides --ms), `--min-iterations` (default 1), `--samples` (default 100), `--parallel` (collect samples concurrently — for async benches).
- Statistics: `--alpha` (significance level, default 0.05 = 95% CI), `--bootstrap` (resamples, default 1000), `--seed` (bootstrap RNG seed; auto-recorded if omitted), `--correction` (post-hoc multiple-comparison method: `none`/`holm`/`bonferroni`, default `holm`), `--verbose` (show test statistics + critical values).
- Output: `--json <file>` (write schema-v1 results), `--label <text>` (annotate the JSON), `-H, --host` / `--host-name <name>` (record machine in the JSON), `--histogram` + `--chart columns|bars` + `--bins N` (per-function distribution chart), `--no-emoji` (ASCII F/S markers), `--observe` (emit User Timing marks at phase boundaries).
- Misc: `--export` (default "default"), `--self`.

### nano-bench-compare

Reads results JSON only (never benchmarks): renders one saved run, or compares several — recomputing significance from the saved samples and warning when the runs' environments differ. Default pairs same-named series across files (before/after); `--pooled` runs one k-sample omnibus over all series.

```bash
npx nano-bench-compare results.json                 # render one saved run
npx nano-bench-compare base.json new.json           # before/after, paired by name
npx nano-bench-compare base.json new.json --pooled  # one omnibus over all series
```

Options: `--alpha` (override the recompute α; default the first file's), `--correction` (override; default the first file's), `--pooled`, `--verbose`, `--histogram` + `--chart` + `--bins`, `--no-emoji`.

### nano-watch

Continuously benchmarks a single function in streaming mode, showing live stats (mean, stdDev, median, skewness, kurtosis, ops/sec) and memory usage.

```bash
npx nano-watch benchmark.js
npx nano-watch benchmark.js methodName
```

Options: `--ms` (measurement time, default 500), `--iterations` (default Infinity), `--export` (default "default"), `--self`.

## Benchmark file format

Both tools import a module. `nano-bench` expects an object of functions; `nano-watch` can use a single function or an object with a method name argument. Each function takes `n` (iteration count) and runs the measured code in a loop:

```js
export default {
  variant1: n => {
    for (let i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
      // measured code
    }
  },
  variant2: n => {
    for (let i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
      // measured code
    }
  }
};
```

## Deno and Bun support

Use `--self` to get the script path for running with alternative interpreters:

```bash
bun `npx nano-bench --self` benchmark.js
deno run -A `npx nano-bench --self` benchmark.js
```

## Statistical methods

- **Bootstrap-percentile CI of the median** — the reported median and its confidence interval are the mean of weighted quantiles over `--bootstrap` resamples; seeded (`--seed`, else auto-recorded) so a recompute reproduces it exactly.
- **Mann-Whitney U test** — two functions; normal approximation, two-sided, tie-corrected. No multiplicity correction (a single comparison).
- **Kruskal-Wallis H test** — three or more functions; beta approximation for the null of H. If significant, **Conover-Iman** pairwise post-hoc (Fisher's LSD on ranks) identifies which pairs differ.
- **Multiple-comparison correction** — `--correction` controls the family-wise error rate over the post-hoc pairwise comparisons: `holm` (default; step-down, uniformly more powerful than Bonferroni), `bonferroni` (single-step), or `none` (uncorrected). Scoped to one omnibus's k(k-1)/2 pairs; the CI's α is untouched.
- **Kolmogorov-Smirnov test** — two-sample distribution comparison; available in the library API (`src/significance/kstest.js`), not wired into the CLI.
- **Distribution histograms** (`--histogram`) — render each function's sample distribution to reveal multimodality, skew, and outlier tails that a median+CI line hides.
- **Online algorithms** (streaming mean, variance, skewness, kurtosis, approximate median) for continuous monitoring.
